payload.answer: Required eval accounting fields are: token counts (input/output), provider/model IDs (for attribution), estimated price (cost projection), and latency percentiles p50, p90, p95 (performance distribution). These enable cost tracking, reproducibility, budgeting, and user experience assessment.
payload.decision: Provide a concise explanation of the required evaluation accounting fields for model inference: token counts, provider/model IDs, estimated price, and latency percentiles (p50, p90, p95).

entity_resolution.reason: No ambiguous or conflicting entities detected in input; all terms are standard and unambiguous in ML evaluation context.

memory_cards.statement: Token counts measure input and output tokens used during model inference, essential for cost estimation and usage tracking.
Provider and model IDs uniquely identify the service and specific model version used, enabling reproducibility and billing attribution.
Estimated price calculates the expected cost of inference based on token usage and provider pricing models, critical for budgeting and optimization.

relationships.object: response time distribution for SLA and UX evaluation
relationships.subject: latency percentiles

memory_cards.statement: Latency percentiles (p50, p90, p95) describe response time distributions: p50 is median latency, p90 and p95 indicate tail latency for SLA and user experience evaluation.

details: No deprecated or conflicting information included.
